evals.name: gotcha-heavy-schema
evals.prompt:
Create a Vespa schema called 'listing' for a real estate search app. Write it to schemas/listing.sd. Requirements:
- address_text (free-text searchable with BM25 support for relevance ranking)
- city (filterable with exact string matching, must be fast)
- price (numeric, filterable and sortable)
- is_active (boolean, used purely as a filter that should not affect relevance scoring)
- description (searchable text, should support BM25, but the full text is large so it should use a dynamic summary to show just a snippet in results)
- embedding (384-dim float vector for semantic search with HNSW using prenormalized-angular distance)
- raw_html (opaque binary blob, stored but never searchable)
- tags (weighted set of strings with integer weights)
- metadata (map from string keys to string values, keys must be filterable with exact match)
- a default fieldset over address_text and description
- a hybrid rank profile that uses BM25 on text fields in first-phase, then combines with closeness() in second-phase using normalize_linear
evals.expected_output: Schema with: enable-bm25 on address_text AND description, city as attribute with fast-search (NOT index+match:exact since that's a gotcha), is_active bool with attribute + fast-search + rank:filter, description with summary:dynamic, embedding tensor with prenormalized-angular distance-metric (NOT fast-search on dense tensor — that's a gotcha), raw_html as raw type with summary only, tags as weightedset<string>, metadata map with struct-field key having match:exact, hybrid rank profile with normalize_linear in second-phase.
evals.llm_rubric:
Grade this Vespa schema for subtle best practices. These are the key differentiators:
(1) Does is_active bool have 'rank: filter'? This is critical — booleans used purely as filters should not contribute to scoring.
(2) Does description have 'summary: dynamic' for snippet generation?
(3) Is the embedding field using prenormalized-angular (NOT angular)? Does it AVOID 'fast-search' on the dense tensor (gotcha #12 — fast-search only works on tensors with at least a mapped dimension)?
(4) Is city using attribute + fast-search for exact matching (NOT index + match:exact which is gotcha #2)?
(5) Is raw_html type 'raw' with 'indexing: summary' only?
(6) Do ALL fields used in bm25() have 'index: enable-bm25' — including description, not just address_text?
(7) Are the map struct-field keys using 'match: exact'?
(8) Does the hybrid rank profile use normalize_linear to combine BM25 and closeness in a second-phase?

evals.name: fix-subtle-bugs
evals.expected_output: Fixed schema addressing: (1) match:exact on attribute-only sku — needs index or use fast-search approach, (2) fast-access on bool is questionable but not invalid — however fast-search is more appropriate, (3) fast-search on dense tensor — not supported (gotcha #12), (4) content field has attribute:paged but bm25(content) requires index — field needs indexing:index, (5) weightedset<float> is invalid — must be string/int/long (gotcha #9), (6) bm25(title) without enable-bm25, (7) fieldset includes content but content is attribute-only — fieldset fields should be indexed.

assertions.text: enable-bm25 added to fields used in bm25()
weightedset uses valid element type (not float)
content field has index in indexing pipeline

evals.llm_rubric:
This schema has 6+ subtle bugs. Grade how many were found:
(1) match:exact on attribute-only sku field has no effect (gotcha #2) — needs index added, or switch to fast-search approach
(2) fast-search on a dense tensor (embedding) is not supported (gotcha #12) — only works on tensors with at least one mapped dimension
(3) weightedset<float> is invalid (gotcha #9) — element type must be string, int, or long
(4) bm25(title) and bm25(content) used without enable-bm25 on either field (gotcha #1)
(5) content field is attribute-only but used in bm25() and fieldset — needs index in its indexing pipeline
(6) fieldset 'default' includes content which is attribute-only — all fieldset members should be indexed strings
Also check: did the fix introduce NEW bugs (e.g., adding fast-access to tensor which is also not supported — gotcha #11)?

evals.name: large-scale-schema-design
evals.prompt:
I'm designing a Vespa schema for a job search platform with 200M documents. Write it to schemas/job.sd. Requirements:
- job_title and job_description (full-text searchable with BM25 ranking)
- company_name (both searchable via text AND filterable as exact match)
- location_city and location_country (filterable, exact match, high-cardinality ~50K cities)
- salary_min and salary_max (numeric range filters)
- posted_date (long timestamp, sortable, used for freshness ranking)
- is_remote (boolean filter, should not affect scoring)
- required_skills (array of strings, filterable)
- seniority_level (string enum: junior/mid/senior/lead, filterable)
- embedding (768-dim float, pre-normalized vectors, for semantic search)
- company_description (large text, stored for display but NOT searchable — too expensive to index at this scale)

Design the schema with memory efficiency in mind — 200M docs means we need to be careful about what goes in memory. Include a rank profile that combines BM25 + freshness + semantic in a phased approach.
evals.expected_output: Schema optimized for scale: company_description with summary only (not indexed), attribute:paged on large rarely-filtered attributes, fast-search on high-frequency filter fields, rank:filter on is_remote, prenormalized-angular on embedding, enable-bm25 on searchable text, proper phased ranking with freshness(posted_date) in expressions.
evals.llm_rubric:
Evaluate this schema for a 200M document deployment:
(1) Is company_description stored with 'indexing: summary' only (not indexed, too expensive at scale)?
(2) Is 'attribute: paged' used on large or rarely-accessed attribute fields to reduce memory pressure (gotcha #5)?
(3) Is is_remote bool using 'rank: filter' to avoid scoring overhead?
(4) Is the embedding using prenormalized-angular (not angular) since vectors are pre-normalized? Does it AVOID fast-search on the dense tensor?
(5) Does company_name have BOTH index (for text search) and attribute + fast-search (for exact filter)? This dual-mode is the correct pattern.
(6) Are high-cardinality filter fields (location_city, location_country) using attribute + fast-search?
(7) Does the rank profile use freshness(posted_date) and does posted_date have attribute indexing?
(8) Is seniority_level using attribute + fast-search with match:word or match:exact (not index — it's an enum)?

evals.name: parent-child-with-ranking
evals.prompt:
Create a parent-child schema setup for a multi-tenant SaaS search platform. Write to schemas/tenant.sd and schemas/document.sd

Parent 'tenant':
- tenant_id (string, unique identifier)
- plan_tier (string enum: free/pro/enterprise)
- boost_factor (double, used to boost search results for paid tenants)

Child 'document':
- title (string, full-text searchable with BM25)
- body (string, full-text searchable with BM25)
- created_at (long timestamp)
- access_level (string, filterable exact match)
- embedding (512-dim float vector, HNSW with dotproduct distance)

The child should import tenant's boost_factor and plan_tier. Create a rank profile that:
1. First-phase: BM25 on title weighted 3x + body
2. Second-phase: combines text score with semantic score, multiplied by the imported boost_factor
evals.expected_output: Two schemas: tenant with all fields as attributes (parent fields must be attributes for import), document with reference<tenant> as attribute-only (no index — gotcha #10), import field statements outside document block, rank profile using imported boost_factor, enable-bm25 on title and body, dotproduct distance-metric on embedding.

assertions.text: Tenant fields use attribute (required for import)

evals.llm_rubric:
Grade these parent-child schemas for correctness:
(1) Is the reference field in document using attribute-ONLY indexing (no index — gotcha #10)?
(2) Are import field statements placed OUTSIDE the document block (common structural mistake)?
(3) Do ALL tenant fields that will be imported use 'attribute' indexing (required for import to work)?
(4) Does the rank profile multiply by the imported boost_factor (attribute(boost_factor) or attribute(tenant_boost_factor))?
(5) Is dotproduct the distance-metric on the embedding (not angular — dotproduct is correct for max inner product search)?
(6) Do title and body both have 'index: enable-bm25'?
(7) Does the first-phase weight title 3x: bm25(title) * 3 + bm25(body)?
(8) Does the second-phase use normalize_linear to combine text and semantic scores before multiplying by boost_factor?

evals.name: complex-field-types
evals.prompt:
Create a Vespa schema called 'event' for a conference/event search platform. Write to schemas/event.sd. Requirements:
- event_name (searchable text with BM25)
- organizer (string, both searchable and filterable)
- venue type should be a struct with fields: name (string), city (string), country (string), capacity (int). The city and country should be filterable with exact match.
- start_date and end_date (long timestamps, sortable, filterable with range queries)
- ticket_prices (a map from string ticket-type names to double prices, the ticket-type keys must be filterable)
- speakers (array of strings, filterable)
- topic_tags (weighted set of strings with relevance weights)
- geo_location (position type for geographic search)
- event_description (large text, searchable with BM25 but use dynamic summary for snippets)
- flyer_image (binary blob, stored for retrieval only)

Include a rank profile that combines BM25 text relevance with geo-distance proximity and freshness.
evals.expected_output: Schema with: struct for venue with struct-field indexing on city/country with match:exact, map with struct-field key indexing, weightedset<string> for topic_tags, position type for geo_location with attribute, raw type for flyer_image with summary-only indexing, enable-bm25 on searchable fields, dynamic summary on event_description, rank profile using distance() for geo and freshness() for dates.

assertions.text: Map ticket-type keys indexed via struct-field

evals.llm_rubric:
Grade this schema for correct use of complex Vespa field types:
(1) Is venue defined as a struct inside the document block, with struct-field declarations for city and country using 'match: exact'?
(2) Is ticket_prices a map<string, double> with struct-field key having attribute + match:exact?
(3) Is topic_tags weightedset<string> (not weightedset<float> or array — gotcha #9)?
(4) Is geo_location type 'position' with 'indexing: attribute | summary' (position cannot use index)?
(5) Is flyer_image type 'raw' with 'indexing: summary' only (raw is not searchable — gotcha #8)?
(6) Does event_description have 'summary: dynamic' for snippet generation?
(7) Do ALL fields used in bm25() have 'index: enable-bm25'?
(8) Does the rank profile use distance(field, geo_location) or closeness(field, geo_location) for geographic ranking?
(9) Are struct-field declarations on the venue field (not inside the struct definition)?
